# numis — Natural Language Money Parser for JavaScript
# License: MIT (https://github.com/ritvij14/numis/blob/main/LICENSE)

## Overview
numis is a lightweight JavaScript/TypeScript library that parses monetary expressions from free-form text into structured data ({ amount, currency }).

## Key Capabilities
- Currency symbols ($, €, £, ¥, ₹ and 70+ more)
- ISO-4217 codes (USD, EUR, GBP)
- Worded numbers ("one hundred dollars")
- Magnitude suffixes ("10k", "5M")
- Slang ("bucks", "quid", "fiver", "grand")
- Ranges ("$50 to $100", "between 100 and 200 EUR")
- Comparison bounds ("< 30k", "> 2 Million")
- Regional formats (European, Swiss, Brazilian)
- Negative values and minor units (cents, pence)

## Quick Start
npm install numis
import { parseMoney } from "numis";
const result = parseMoney("The price is $49.99");
// { original: "The price is $49.99", amount: 49.99, currency: "USD" }

## Documentation
Full docs and API reference: https://numis.ritvij.dev/#documentation
GitHub repository: https://github.com/ritvij14/numis
npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/numis
